summary: Reviewed the live-event reliability changes across admin game/session CRUD, display/player state freshness, connection health, and migrations. The strongest risks are stale payloads still mutating derived UI, state_version being treated as globally comparable across games, and deployment-time assumptions around timing/reset data. Overall the direction is sound, but several assumptions should be fixed or explicitly decided before relying on this for the next live event.
appears_sound: Proxy matcher remains narrowly scoped to `/admin/:path*`, `/host/:path*`, and `/login` in `src/proxy.ts:8`, preserving public display/player performance.
`state_version` is added to both `game_states` and `game_states_public`, and the sync trigger copies it in `supabase/migrations/20260430120000_add_state_version.sql:5`.
Prize requirements are centralized in `validateGamePrizes` and tested for standard, snowball, jackpot, and whitespace cases in `src/lib/prize-validation.test.ts:11`.
Invite-only auth is preserved: `signup()` always fails in `src/app/login/actions.ts:31`, and the login UI only exposes Sign In.
No changed migration adds a uniqueness constraint to `winners`, so multi-winner support appears preserved.

findings.description: The `state_version` freshness gate only protects `currentGameState`; `currentPrizeText` is updated unconditionally from the incoming payload. A stale lower-version payload can be ignored for state but still roll the displayed prize/stage text back on display/player.
findings.evidence: DisplayUI calls `setCurrentGameState(...isFreshGameState...)` and then derives `setCurrentPrizeText` from `incoming.current_stage_index`; PlayerUI shows the same pattern at `src/app/player/[sessionId]/player-ui.tsx:176`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: If stale payloads never occur, this path is harmless, but the change explicitly assumes Realtime and polling can arrive out of order.
findings.what_would_confirm: Replay a newer state followed by an older payload and assert prize text stays aligned with the newer state.

findings.description: Freshness assumes `state_version` alone identifies ordering, but versions are per game row and reset to 0 for new games. After an active-game switch, a delayed old-game snapshot with a higher version can pass the comparator and replace the new game's state.
findings.evidence: `isFreshGameState` compares only `state_version`, and DisplayUI applies `payload.new` without checking `incoming.game_id` against the active game at `src/app/display/[sessionId]/display-ui.tsx:174`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: Supabase channel filtering and channel cleanup may normally prevent old-game payloads after a switch.
findings.what_would_confirm: Simulate switching `active_game_id` and then delivering a delayed previous-game payload with a higher `state_version`.

findings.description: Started-game edit UI disables the fieldset containing locked structural fields, so those values are not submitted with the form. The server then treats the missing type/stage/prize values as attempted structural changes, blocking otherwise allowed metadata edits.
findings.evidence: `<fieldset disabled={isGameLocked}>` wraps type/stages/prizes, while `updateGame` reads those fields from `FormData` and compares them to the original at `src/app/admin/sessions/[id]/actions.ts:158`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: If the product intends started games to be fully read-only, the failure is expected, but the project rule says only prize/type/snowball/stage fields are locked.
findings.what_would_confirm: Open a started game, change only notes or background colour, and submit; the server should not return a locked-field error.

findings.description: The prize lock uses `JSON.stringify` for semantic JSON map equality, which assumes object key order is stable across a Postgres `jsonb` round trip. An unchanged prize map can be rejected as changed if key order differs.
findings.evidence: `originalPrizes` is taken from the stored JSON value and compared with `JSON.stringify(prizes) !== JSON.stringify(originalPrizes)`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The current Supabase response may happen to return keys in the same order as the rebuilt object.
findings.what_would_confirm: Round-trip a standard game's prizes through Supabase and compare the stringified stored value against the `FormData`-rebuilt object in stage order.

findings.description: Connection health can enter the reconnect/auto-refresh path from sustained polling failures even while Realtime payloads are still arriving. Realtime data delivery is not recorded as a success event, so a healthy live subscription cannot clear the unhealthy state after the initial `SUBSCRIBED` status.
findings.evidence: `poll-failure` calls `flipUnhealthy`, while the DisplayUI Realtime payload handler updates state but does not call any health success marker at `src/app/display/[sessionId]/display-ui.tsx:171`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: A later poll success clears the state, so this only surfaces when polling is broken for more than the banner/refresh thresholds.
findings.what_would_confirm: Mock poll failures for 31 seconds while injecting Realtime updates and assert the banner does not auto-refresh.

findings.description: The final call-delay backfill rewrites every row whose delay is not 2, including live rows or intentionally configured non-default pacing. That is a product decision hidden inside a migration and can change host/display timing during deployment.
findings.evidence: The migration runs `where call_delay_seconds <> 2` against both `game_states` and `game_states_public`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The intended event-wide policy may be that all existing rows must be forced to two seconds.
findings.what_would_confirm: Ask whether any existing or in-progress game may keep a non-2 `call_delay_seconds`; if yes, restrict the backfill to known legacy defaults or non-live rows.

findings.description: The reset flow visibly deletes game state and winner audit rows, but the pack does not show rollback of snowball pot state/history. If reset is used after a snowball pot was rolled over or awarded, the session can be erased while the cross-session jackpot remains mutated.
findings.evidence: `resetSession` deletes `game_states` and `winners`, while `handleSnowballPotUpdate` mutates `snowball_pots` and `snowball_pot_history` in `src/app/host/actions.ts:126`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The omitted tail of `resetSession` may compensate for pot/history, or reset may intentionally not undo jackpot carryover.
findings.what_would_confirm: Inspect the full `resetSession` implementation and run a snowball win/rollover then reset-session scenario against expected pot values.

empty_categories: No proxy matcher broadening found; public display, player, and root routes still bypass the auth proxy.
No public sign-up affordance found in the changed login UI.
No changed migration adds a winners uniqueness constraint or obvious multi-winner dedupe path.
No `Date.now()` impurity found in the pure connection-health reducer; time is injected into reducer/selectors and tested with fixed epochs.
